reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 1 (Dead Code): PASSED — Function inclusionProofKey is actively called by SidecarInclusionProven (L366), which is part of GossipDataColumnSidecarRequirements used for P2P gossip validation. Gate 2 (Trust Boundary): PASSED — The bug is in gossip validation (GossipDataColumnSidecarRequirements), which processes untrusted P2P data. Attack path is via P2P network packets, not trusted EL/Engine API. Gate 3 (Code Verification): PASSED — Code reading is factually correct. The cache key at beacon-chain/verification/data_column.go:527-547 constructs a 160-byte key from: (1) KzgCommitmentsInclusionProof [128 bytes, L539-543], (2) SignedBlockHeader root [32 bytes, L534-545]. The KzgCommitments field (the actual data being proven) is NOT included in the key. Two DataColumnSidecars with identical inclusion proofs and headers but different KzgCommitments will produce the same cache key, causing the second to bypass Merkle verification (L368-370). Gate 4 (Exploitability): PASSED — An attacker can trigger this via P2P gossip by sending: (1) valid DataColumnSidecar A with valid inclusion proof, (2) malicious DataColumnSidecar M with same proof/header but forged KzgCommitments. The cache lookup succeeds (L368), causing M to skip full verification (L375) and be accepted with invalid commitments. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): 01e entry PROP-6a4369e9-inv-042 in outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json:1060-1076 states: 'Any cached/optimized inclusion proof verification must match full Merkle branch verification.' The code violates this by using a cache key that omits the commitments, allowing cached results to be reused for different data. Gate 6 (Scope Check): PASSED — Finding is in-scope for Fusaka PeerDAS implementation. Severity: Critical — Accepting invalid KZG commitments allows an attacker to propagate malicious data columns that would fail full verification, potentially causing consensus splits or data availability failures affecting the entire network. No client diversity cap applies (bug affects all nodes receiving the malicious data via P2P). An attacker can trigger this via P2P gossip by crafting DataColumnSidecar M with valid inclusion proof (copied from a legitimate sidecar) but malicious KzgCommitments. When M arrives after a legitimate sidecar with the same proof/header, the cache hit causes M to skip verification, causing acceptance of invalid commitments and propagation to peers.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Any cached/optimized inclusion proof verification must match full Merkle branch verification. (forall proof: optimized_merkle_verify(proof) == is_valid_merkle_branch(proof))
